ccel.title: ANF07. Fathers of the Third and Fourth Centuries: Lactantius, Venantius, Asterius, Victorinus, Dionysius, Apostolic Teaching and Constitutions, Homily, and Liturgies

source.title: ANF07. Fathers of the Third and Fourth Centuries: Lactantius, Venantius, Asterius, Victorinus, Dionysius, Apostolic Teaching and Constitutions, Homily, and Liturgies

title: Chapter XIII.—Support of Prophets

source.title: ANF07. Fathers of the Third and Fourth Centuries: Lactantius, Venantius, Asterius, Victorinus, Dionysius, Apostolic Teaching and Constitutions, Homily, and Liturgies

verses.book: The Teaching of the Twelve Apostles
verses.text: But every true prophet that willeth to abide among you is worthy of his support.

verses.book: The Teaching of the Twelve Apostles
verses.text: So also a true teacher is himself worthy, as the workman, of his support.

verses.book: The Teaching of the Twelve Apostles
verses.text: Every first-fruit, therefore, of the products of wine-press and threshing-floor, of oxen and of sheep, thou shalt take and give to the prophets, for they are your high priests.

verses.book: The Teaching of the Twelve Apostles
verses.text: But if ye have not a prophet, give it to the poor.

verses.book: The Teaching of the Twelve Apostles
verses.text: If thou makest a batch of dough, take the first-fruit and give according to the commandment.

verses.book: The Teaching of the Twelve Apostles
verses.text: So also when thou openest a jar of wine or of oil, take the first-fruit and give it to the prophets; 7. and of money (silver) and clothing and every possession, take the first-fruit, as it may seem good to thee, and give according to the commandment.
